preamble: In the process of Tonogenesis, the debuccalization of a final sibilant [s] into a glottal fricative [h] represents a critical intermediate stage. According to Haudricourt's model, this Laryngeal Coda originally triggered a lowering of the vowel's Pitch Contour, which eventually became phonemicized as a falling tone (Qù sheng or sắc/nặng).
phoneticEffects: Debuccalization, Tonogenesis, Pitch Fall, Voice Loss

examples.note: Historical final -s in Mon-Khmer was first debuccalized to -h and then elided, giving rise to the 'Sắc' (high-falling) tone in Vietnamese.
The 'Departing' category (Qù sheng) in Middle Chinese is reconstructed as having a final [s] (later [h]) which influenced the Pitch Contour of the syllable.
Tai-Kadai languages show a similar pattern where final spirants debuccalized, contributing to the development of the falling tone register.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Baxter, W. H. (1992). A Handbook of Old Chinese Phonology.
